rationale: The cited paper (2009) focuses on subjective time perception, duration salience, and their effects on intertemporal discounting, while the citing paper (2019) tests an attention-to-delay  anticipated-duration mechanism for shifts in present bias in intertemporal choice. This is a close mechanism-level and construct-level overlap (not just topical background), and the reference is an exact DOI match, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
